title: oxalis polyphylla
intro: Oxalis polyphylla, also known as the purple wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalidaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant contains oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including treating skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Oxalis polyphylla include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and gout. Oxalis polyphylla is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in herbal infusions to leverage its medicinal and health benefits.
